row.column: Disables the autocomplete — it will not respond to user input. Pass true to also clear the input value. auto.disable(); auto.disable(true); Example: disable
Re-enables the autocomplete after disable() or destroy(). Restores all event listeners without triggering a search. auto.enable(); Example: enable/disable toggle, enable/disable with empty input
Clears the input and closes the results list while keeping the autocomplete fully functional. Use this instead of destroy() for a clear button or any programmatic reset. auto.reset();
Clears the input and removes all event listeners. Use only for permanent teardown (e.g. closing a modal). auto.destroy();
Re-renders the results without modifying the input field. Optionally pass a string to also update the input value. auto.rerender(); auto.rerender('search term');
